what_it_does: Defines a React Brand Kit screen with logo upload placeholders, a company name input that updates a URL preview, a hard-coded colour scheme list, and a styled preview panel.
observations: Uses React useState aliased as useState_bk from the global React object.
Defines a small ColourRow helper for swatch-and-label rows.
Company name state defaults to "Acme Architecture" and is transformed into a lowercase hyphenated URL segment inline.
The screen uses CSS custom properties and cv-* class names alongside many inline style objects.
Upload areas are visual dropzones with plus signs, with no file handling in this file.
The preview panel contains title text, emoji-based amenity icons, description copy, and a primary button.
Exports BrandKit by assigning it to window.BrandKit.
standouts: Company name input drives a live URL preview string.
Colour scheme values are presented as explicit hard-coded rows.
Brand preview composes text, icons, description, and button styling into one compact sample.
conventions: Uses global React and global window assignment rather than module imports or exports.
Uses design-system class names prefixed with cv-.
Mixes reusable classes with file-local inline layout and typography styles.
Uses British spelling in ColourRow and Colour Scheme labels.
concerns: Repeated inline styles make layout and visual changes harder to centralize within this file.
Colour rows are hard-coded one by one instead of rendered from a data array.
The URL slug transform only lowercases and replaces whitespace, leaving punctuation and empty values unhandled.
Two-column grid layouts are fixed in this file and do not show responsive handling here.
Emoji icons in the preview have no accessible labels in this file.
notes: This file is a self-contained Brand Kit UI mockup/screen focused on brand assets, naming, colours, and preview output.
questions: Are the logo dropzones intended to receive upload behavior from another layer?
Are colour rows meant to be editable controls or display-only swatches?
Should the company-name URL segment sanitize punctuation, leading/trailing separators, or empty input?
Is the preview meant to reflect the listed colour values dynamically?
purpose_vs_actual: The screen title and implementation align: it renders brand-kit controls for logos, company naming, colour scheme display, and preview.
intention_signals: It appears intended to show how a customer can configure brand-facing tour presentation details, with immediate visual context for names, logos, colours, and output styling.
novelty: The file embeds a live URL preview beside the company-name input and pairs static brand settings with a miniature branded presentation preview.
contribution: Contributes a brand-configuration screen pattern with logo placeholders, company identity editing, colour swatch rows, and a compact preview composition that a fused system could retain as a brand-kit substrate.
summary_for_embedding: React JSX file defining a Brand Kit screen with logo dropzones, company-name state, URL preview generation, hard-coded colour swatches, and a branded preview panel.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a brand identity setup interface where users can see how logos, naming, colours, and presentation styling would appear.

symbols.does: Renders a single colour swatch row with a label.
symbols.description: ColourRow is a presentational helper component that receives a color value and label, then displays a square swatch and text in a flex row. It relies on inline styles and shared CSS variables for spacing, border, radius, font, and foreground color.

symbols.does: Renders the full Brand Kit screen and manages the company name used in the URL preview.
symbols.description: BrandKit is the screen component for brand asset setup, containing sections for main logo, company name, additional logos, colour scheme, and preview. It stores the company name in local React state, updates it from an input, and derives a lowercase hyphenated URL preview directly in JSX.
